data.title: Bridgewater’s Ray Dalio: Market has entered bubble territory, but investors shouldn’t sell
data.description:
Bridgewater Associates founder Ray Dalio believes the current market has reached bubble territory, warning that we’re at about “80%” of the conditions that defined previous market bubbles, like those seen in 1929 and 2000.

In an interview with CNBC, Dalio explained that despite these warning signs, investors shouldn’t necessarily rush to sell their holdings.

“There’s definitely a bubble in markets,” Dalio said, defining a bubble as a situation where “there’s a lot of creation of wealth” through mechanisms like inflated valuations and excessive multiples.

The American billionaire and hedge-fund manager noted the current concentration of wealth and leverage in the market as key indicators of bubble conditions.

Dalio emphasized that timing is critical when considering bubble dynamics. “Don’t sell just because there’s a bubble,” he advised, while noting that historical data shows investments made during similar high valuation periods typically yield poor returns over the following decade, ranging between “2% and -2% a year over 10 years.”

The investment veteran explained the mechanics of how bubbles eventually burst, pointing out that they require a triggering event.

“Bubbles have to be pricked,” he said, identifying that the “need for cash” typically serves as the catalyst when investors must sell assets to raise money. While monetary tightening often serves as this trigger, Dalio noted, “we’re not going to have that now.”

According to Dalio’s analysis, market vulnerability currently stems from ownership patterns and leverage. He distinguished between “strong hands” (typically company owners investing their own money) and “weak hands” (often leveraged retail investors), suggesting the concentration of assets in potentially unstable ownership structures contributes significantly to market fragility.

“We are in that bubble territory,” Dalio concluded, “but we don’t have the pricking of the bubble yet.”

Dow ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (UDOW), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW)

data.title: Nasdaq pops +2% at the open after Nvidia results, strong Sept jobs report
data.description:
U.S. stocks soared at the open on Thursday after Nvidia's (NVDA) earnings flipped the script on risk, at least for this session. Meanwhile, the much-delayed September jobs report finally arrived, blowing past estimates for nonfarm payroll growth. 

The tech-heavy Nasdsaq Composite (COMP:IND) led the charge, jumping +2.1%. The benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) gained +1.6%, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) added +1.4%. 

Nvidia was +3.7% after the opening bell, after adding more than $200B to its market cap with its post-earnings rally, nearing the $200/share level it lost early this month.

Thursday's spotlight was on the September nonfarm payrolls report, which was initially supposed to come out on October 3. However, the longest U.S. government shutdown in history derailed that release. 

The data showed the U.S. labor market added 119K jobs in September, easily outstripping the consensus of 50K. The unemployment rate ticked up to 4.4% in the month, a tad higher than the expected 4.3%.   

Looking at the fixed-income markets, Treasury yields turned lower after the jobs report, as traders snapped up bonds.

data.title: USA Rare Earth, Solvay partner to provide rare earth materials for U.S. magnet production
data.description:
USA Rare Earth (USAR) +1.1% in early trading Thursday after saying it has established a strategic partnership with chemical company Solva (OTCPK:SVYSF) (OTCPK:SLVYY) to supply rare earth metals to Permag, a leader in high-precision magnets and magnetic assemblies.

Under the agreement, USA Rare Earth's (USAR) newly acquired subsidiary Less Common Metals will leverage its metallization expertise to produce samarium materials using oxides concentrated and separated by Solvay (OTCPK:SVYSF) (OTCPK:SLVYY), enabling Permag to meet rising demand for Samarium magnets over the next 3-5 years.

Samarium is a critical input for high-performance magnets and assemblies essential to advanced manufacturing, defense, and clean-energy technologies.

data.title: Solventum sets $1 billion share repurchase program
data.description:
• Solventum (SOLV) board has approved a share repurchase program, which authorizes the company to purchase up to $1 billion of its outstanding common stock.
  • Solventum expects to begin repurchasing shares in 2026 and had ~173.4 million shares outstanding as of October 31, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Solventum to buy private wound care firm Acera for up to $850M
data.description:
Solventum (SOLV) 3M’s (MMM) healthcare spinoff, on Thursday announced an agreement to acquire Acera Surgical, a privately owned wound care company, in an all-cash deal worth up to $850M.

Headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri, Acera makes synthetic treatments for soft tissue repair. Backed by its Restrata wound care products, the company is expected to generate roughly $90M in sales this year.

Solventum (SOLV) said it will utilize its cash on hand to fund the deal, which involves an upfront payment of $725M and up to $125M in additional payments pending the achievement of certain milestones.

The transaction is expected to close in H1 2026 and be slightly dilutive to the company's adjusted EPS in 2026 and accretive to adjusted EPS from 2027.

Concurrently, Maplewood, Minnesota-based Solventum (SOLV) announced a new share repurchase program to buy back up to $1B of its common stock starting in 2026.

data.title: Cemtrex to acquire Invocon in $7.06 million deal
data.description:
• Cemtrex (CETX) has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire systems-engineering firm Invocon, for $7.06 million, the company said on Thursday.
  • Invocon’s technologies have been deployed across satellites, launch vehicles, target missiles, space shuttles, and the International Space Station, including 17 shuttle systems, 10 ISS systems, and numerous next-generation flight programs for major primes and government agencies.
  • Invocon also holds a deep intellectual property portfolio, including multiple U.S. patents related to hypervelocity impact detection, gridless lethality assessment, acoustic and RF sensing, and advanced flight-system telemetry.
  • The transaction is expected to close on or around January 1, 2026.
  • CETX -26.99% premarket to $2.65.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: HP sees price target trimmed at Morgan Stanley ahead of earnings
data.description:
Morgan Stanley cut the price target on HP Inc.'s (HPQ) stock to $21 from $24 while previewing the upcoming earnings of enterprise hardware original equipment manufacturers.

HP is slated to report its fiscal fourth quarter results on Nov. 25, while Pure Storage (PSTG) is scheduled to report its third quarter results on Dec. 2. 

"Earnings unlikely to settle memory cost debate, but still creates a broadly challenging setup across the board with PSTG best positioned vs. HPQ most at-risk," said analysts led by Erik Woodring.

The analysts noted that their checks suggest enterprise hardware spending is tracking in line to slightly below expectations, calendar fourth quarter to-date, with a modest softening versus prior checks, largely due to the timing of the U.S. Federal Government shutdown. Beyond the demand environment, the analysts' concern is the impact that rising memory prices will have on OEM margins, which they don’t necessarily expect to materialize this quarter (likely in the first half of calendar 2026), but which still poses a growing risk to calendar year 2026 earnings.

"As a result, we’re broadly cautious on the earnings setups for all Hardware OEMs this quarter, but would expect EW-rated PSTG to be most-insulated/best-positioned amongst the group. Conversely, we see the greatest risk of negative EPS revisions at HPQ, where our FY26 EPS is 11% below consensus," said Woodring and his team.

data.title: Axalta holder Artisan Partners strongly opposes Akzo deal
data.description:
Axalta Coating (AXTA) holder Artisan Partners said it's against the all-stock merger with Akzo Nobel (OTCQX:AKZOF).

"As an Axalta shareholder, we believe the only proper response to this proposed transaction is an absolute and resounding `NO,'  Artisan Partners said in a statement on Wednesday.

The statement comes after Axalta (AXTA) on Tuesday announced an all-stock merger with Akzo Nobel (OTCQX:AKZOF) to create a global coatings company valued at approximately $25 billion.

"We are struggling to comprehend what is happening here," Artisan Partners said in the statement. "Axalta and Akzo Nobel are calling this a `merger of equals,' but let’s call it what it really is: selling out of a cheap business that is performing well and taking the currency of a company that has never done well, one that has far inferior assets and financial performance," Artisan Partners said in the statement.

data.title: Aris Mining, Colombia reach settlement to end arbitration; takes full ownership of Soto Norte
data.description:
Aris Mining (ARMN) +4% pre-market Thursday after saying it reached a settlement with Colombia's government to end the arbitration proceeding brought under the Canada-Colombia Free Trade Agreement, ending a dispute dating back to 2018.

Under the settlement, no cash payments were made or contemplated, and the agreement is structured around a series of performance obligations by Aris Mining (ARMN) and multiple Colombian government entities.

Separately, Aris (ARMN) said it agreed to acquire the remaining 49% interest in the Soto Norte joint venture in Colombia from MDC Industry Holding for $80 million, comprised of $60 million in cash and nearly 1.74 million newly issued common shares at $11.50/share.

Aris (ARMN) said full ownership of Soto Norte will enhance its ability to advance the project as a core long-term growth asset, and position it to become a 1 million oz/year gold producer.

data.title: Nasdaq upgraded, MarketAxess downgraded at Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Morgan Stanley on Thursday upgraded Nasdaq (NDAQ) to Overweight from Equal-Weight on the basis of both cyclical and secular tailwinds, but also downgraded rival MarketAxess Holdings (MKTX) to Equal-Weight from Overweight.

Such tailwinds are expected to drive accelerate revenue growth across Nasdaq's (NDAQ) Solutions businesses into 2026-2027, analyst Michael Cyprys wrote of Nasdaq in a note.

"We see NDAQ as an attractive play on capital markets recovery plus a business transformation story on their shift into higher quality, more recurring revenue streams in large addressable markets," he explained. A revival in dealmaking is set to boost listing fees, "while a supportive macro backdrop for risk assets into 2026 should lift index revenues and support its workflow & insights businesses."

Meanwhile, MarketAxess (MKTX) shares were downgraded due to a potentially benign credit outlook that could weigh on secondary trading volumes into" next year," Cyprys wrote, adding macroeconomic headwinds could limit multiple expansion.

Morgan Stanley's Equal-Weight rate on MKTX compares with the average SA analyst rating and the average Wall Street analyst rating, both at Buy, as well as the SA Quant system rating of Sell.

In premarket trading, NDAQ gained 1.9%, while MKTX slipped 0.2%.
